title: Update
body: I loved this app and started talking to so many people and was extremely happy with it UNTIL I accidentally updated it today and it all went downhill. I'm 17, and beforehand I could make my age range whatever I wanted (I personally had it from 17-20, an age range I felt comfortable with). Now, with the update, my only options are 13-17. I thought that it'd be okay and I could just make it 17, but alas you have to have your range stretch over at least 2 ages so it had to be 16-17. And now whenever I swipe through people, almost every single one of them are 16 (I counted: 93 out of 100 people I swiped were 16). Yes, I could change my birth date to a year earlier, but then I wouldn't see any 17 year olds. I just think that it's a lot more realistic that a 17 year old girl would talk to an 18 year old guy than a 16 year old guy, but CLEARLY that cannot be the case. Please change it back

title: It’s good, but...
body: I enjoyed this app for the week or two I’ve had it downloaded, and so far I’ve had very few bad experiences with other people on the app. The thing that I have a big problem with is the ban system. People often make reports just because they don’t feel like waiting 15 seconds for the time to run out so they don’t have to talk to whoever they are chatting with. This has caused my account to be banned and I’ve had to write to support about it. What I think that could help this problem is the addition of a skip button during the first 15 seconds of the call. This would prevent people from simply reporting other innocent users because they are bored or etc.

title: Monkey is a great interactive social app
body:
Monkey is an app you'd download and get a bunch of friends together just to see what happens. It's a new experience every time and you might actually meet a new friend.

This is NOT a dating app, the pictures the have for the app on their app page makes it look like it's only for people who want a quick minute with someone, but you can actually have a pretty good time just talking to people around the world.

You should probably have pretty thick skin going in though, some people are of course going to be mean and hurtful, but most of the time it's friendly confrontation and at the very worst just 10 seconds of teenager harassment.

The idea you have to both agree to keep talking to each other by pressing a button makes this app more special than something like Omegle. The Snapchat feature also keeps the conversation going even after the call ends.

Overall, this is a cool little experience for like a week and then you'll probably never use it again unless you're really bored, but that's the point. This isn't an app you use for a long period, but it's great in short bursts. Have fun Monkeying around!

title: Transphobic
body: Literally as soon as you’re signing up it gives you two options for gender, which is disappointing but not even the worst part. A lot of dating apps still only have two options. Yes, it’s sad they don’t offer an ‘other’ or ‘non binary’ but the absolute shttiest part is once you pick ‘girl’ or ‘boy’ a super passive aggressive notification pops up that says “You can’t change your gender yo 💁‍♀️”. Yes!! They actually use an emoji of a girl flipping her hair. It 100% is a passive aggressive, anti-trans message and I cant say that with confidence because when you’re inputting your username, at the bottom of the username line, it simply says things like ‘usernames need to be at least 3 letters’ or ‘username can’t be changed’ as you’re typing. But when you’re inputting your gender, A WHOLE NOTIFICATION pops up on your phone, as if to REMIND you that transgender people are invalid and unaccepted in the Monkey community. I will be emailing the Monkey team. I will not be using this app, I will encourage my Twitter and Instagram following not to use this app, and I encourage any readers not to use this app.

title: Great App
body: I think the reason I love this app is because it allows people to connect via video chat and with our world being so inclined with technology this app will last for a while. However, what I think should be improved is the amount of time you can add. When you first hit the "Add time" button you should at least get 3 more minutes when adding time for the first time. Next, there should be a feature that allows you to exit from the person you're talking to instead of waiting on the time to run out. Maybe try putting an exit button on the screen when video chatting. However, great app for my first time use.

title: To old
body: Okay so I downloaded the app a couple of weeks ago and everything was good. I’m 15 so I put in my age and ppl around my age group were being requested for me to accept. However, when I recently got on the app, the age group had shifted to those that are 19 and older. I’ve gone on it a few days to see if maybe it was just the time that had affected this but it kept showing me ppl that were 19+ years old. This is a very fun app and I like to use it but I would prefer if ppl around my age would show up as opposed to older ppl.
